title: Build Something
description: The fastest way to learn is to build. Pick a project below, paste the prompts into your AI coding tool, and ship something real this weekend. No experience required — each guide walks you through it step by step.
outcomes: Go from zero to a working app you can share with a link
Understand the basics of how web apps, bots, and extensions work
Learn to iterate on AI-generated code — add features, fix bugs, make it yours
Deploy a real project to the internet

recommendedPaths.title: Build a Todo App
recommendedPaths.reason: The classic first project. Simple enough to finish in an hour, complex enough to learn real concepts like state, storage, and UI.

recommendedPaths.title: Build a Personal Website
recommendedPaths.reason: Create a portfolio site you actually own. Great for anyone who wants a web presence — students, freelancers, job seekers.

recommendedPaths.title: Build a Chrome Extension
recommendedPaths.reason: Customize your browser with a tool you'll actually use every day. A satisfying project that feels immediately useful.

recommendedPaths.title: Build a Discord Bot
recommendedPaths.reason: Build a bot for your server — moderator, game, or utility. Fun to build, fun to show friends, and teaches real API concepts.

concepts.name: Frontend vs Backend
concepts.explanation: Frontend is what users see (HTML, CSS, JavaScript in the browser). Backend is the server-side logic — databases, APIs, authentication. Most projects touch both.

concepts.name: State Management
concepts.explanation: State is the data your app holds in memory — the list of todos, whether a toggle is on or off. Understanding state is the key to understanding how apps behave.

concepts.name: Deployment
concepts.explanation: Deployment means putting your app on the internet so anyone can use it. Services like Vercel, Railway, and Netlify make this free and simple for small projects.

tools.name: Cursor
tools.description: AI-first code editor — the fastest way to build projects with AI assistance

tools.name: Claude Code
tools.description: Agentic coding in your terminal — describe what you want and it builds it

tools.name: Vercel
tools.description: Free hosting for web apps — deploy with one command
